meta.title: What should ShiftMate build?
meta.subtitle: Build-option decision for an hourly shift-swap product
meta.provenance: build-options skill · 5 options × 8 criteria · 3-judge panel + adversarial stress-test (illustrative golden example)
meta.sourceRun: market-validation: faster shift swapping / open-shift coverage for hourly teams

context.constraints: Solo/small team; weeks-to-months horizon; must keep cost-to-serve low and ship a wedge, not a full suite.
incumbents: Full-suite schedulers (When I Work, Deputy) ship swap features; Homebase offers a strong free tier; group-text + spreadsheet is the free status quo → differentiate on liquidity and a vertical, not on being another scheduler

options.name: Open-shift marketplace
options.thesis: Win the one job full-suite tools do clumsily: instantly match open shifts to available workers. Fast matching + push beats a manager's group text, and it's the wedge incumbents under-serve.
options.mvpScope: Worker app + manager dashboard: post open shift → eligible workers pinged → first-accept (with approval rules) → calendar/clock sync. Per-location pricing.
options.businessModel: Per-location monthly floor; upsell on filled-shift volume.
options.incumbentBeat: Suites treat swapping as a checkbox; a liquidity-first marketplace is faster and stickier for the coverage job.

wtp_fit.notes: coverage is the daily pain operators pay for

incumbent_displacement.notes: directly out-executes the weak swap UX

moat.notes: liquidity helps but feature is copyable

asset_fit.notes: reuses the app + data model wholesale

killerRisks: incumbents could improve swap UX and erase the edge
per-location liquidity cold-start may stall growth

options.name: Coverage API / white-label
options.thesis: Sell the matching engine as an API/white-label that POS and workforce apps embed for open-shift filling.
options.mvpScope: Authenticated REST endpoint: post shift + eligibility → ranked candidates; usage-based keys; 1-2 design partners.
options.incumbentBeat: Distribution leverage — ride partners' reach instead of fighting suites direct.

wtp_fit.notes: vendors pay if it saves build

options.name: Compliance system-of-record
options.thesis: Go beyond swapping into a documented system-of-record for predictive-scheduling / fair-workweek compliance, sold at a premium.
options.mvpScope: Add change logs, premium-pay triggers, and audit exports to the swap flow; premium tier.
options.businessModel: Higher per-location or subscription for compliance tier.
options.incumbentBeat: Differentiate on depth where lightweight swap tools are commoditized.

reg_risk.notes: compliance is the product, lower downside

killerRisks: regulated-metro market may be too small to anchor alone

options.name: Freemium worker app
options.thesis: Free worker swap app to acquire teams bottom-up; paid manager dashboard.
options.mvpScope: Free worker swap/claim flow → upsell managers to approvals + analytics.
options.incumbentBeat: Bottom-up worker acquisition the incumbents don't chase.

unit_econ.notes: free tier cost; thin paid margin

killerRisks: free-to-paid conversion may not cover costs

options.name: Bundle with POS / payroll
options.thesis: Bundle scheduling/coverage into a POS or payroll suite as an add-on module.
options.mvpScope: Integration/module + revenue share with a POS or payroll vendor.
options.businessModel: Rev-share / add-on fee via partner software.
options.incumbentBeat: Owns the operator workflow context lightweight tools lack.

killerRisks: partner dependency dictates timeline & take rate

recommendation.rationale: The open-shift marketplace reuses the existing app + shift model, attacks the coverage job incumbents do clumsily, and has the fastest path to revenue with the best asset fit. Its weakness is moat (a swap feature is copyable) — so graft the compliance system-of-record as phase 2 for defensibility.
grafts: Add the compliance system-of-record (compliance-premium) as phase 2 to turn a copyable feature into a sticky system of record (moat).
Layer the coverage API (white-label-api) later for distribution once the matching engine is proven.

killCriteria: If incumbents ship materially better swap UX, the wedge narrows — re-score incumbent_displacement and lean into compliance depth.
If per-location liquidity won't cold-start (not enough available workers to fill posts), the marketplace value fails — shift weight to a vertical with denser staffing.
If operators won't pay above the free tier for coverage alone, attach price to compliance/system-of-record value instead of the swap feature.
